texto: I had never seen her smile before. At least, not beyond the cursory upturn at the edges of her lips as we passed each other in the hallway. But now, her smile transformed her, as if she'd grown taller, or changed her hair color, or something. Her cheeks popped with dimples, her lips seemed redder and softer against the backdrop of her white teeth. Damn. She was cute.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Allen Eskens: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de limites e escolhas conscientes. O trecho “I had never seen her smile before. At least, not beyond the cursory upturn at the edges of her li…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que desenvolvimento-pessoal, smile, transform pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de mudanca e transform.

Her cheeks popped with dimples, her lips seemed redder and softer against the backdrop of her white teeth. Damn. She was cute.
